# sim-run Human Docs

<!-- GENERATED BY xtask simdoc; DO NOT EDIT. -->

This lane contains human-readable guides generated by `xtask simdoc`.

## Index Backlinks

This generated lane consumes `docs/generated/sim-index-fragment.sx`. Global index consumers merge that fragment into `docs/index/overview.md`, `docs/index/features.md`, and the package maps.

| Index row | Kind | Title |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `repo/sim-run` | `repo` | sim-run |
| `doc-set/sim-run/generated` | `doc-set` | sim-run generated docs |

## Feature Rows

| Feature | Subject | Specimens | Summary |
| --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| `feature/sim-run/index` | `crate/xtask` | 1 | Expose generated package, card, surface, and recipe facts as a checked SIM Index fragment. |
| `feature/sim-run/bootloader` | `crate/sim-run-core` | 2 | Start product commands through the shared bootloader and loaded runtime libraries. |
| `feature/sim-run/watch` | `crate/sim-run` | 1 | Start modeled, imported, or live watch plans through the shared command bootloader. |
| `feature/sim-run/glasses` | `crate/sim-run` | 1 | Start modeled or hardware-backed glasses plans through the shared command bootloader. |
| `feature/sim-run/repl` | `crate/sim-lib-repl` | 1 | Run a SIM read-eval-print loop through the loaded REPL library and command surface. |
| `feature/sim-run/runtime-index` | `crate/sim-lib-index` | 4 | Explore the merged SIM Index through the bootloader as stable Table/Dir rows and structured query output. |
| `feature/sim-run/loaders` | `crate/sim-run-loaders` | 1 | Load native, source, and re-exported runtime libraries as bootloader inputs. |
| `feature/sim-run/index-table-dir` | `crate/sim-lib-index` | 1 | Expose the embedded SIM Index as immutable Table/Dir collections for loaded runtime code. |
| `feature/sim-run/terminal-surface` | `crate/sim-view-tty` | 1 | Render and interpret terminal view intents through the loaded TTY surface library. |

### `feature/sim-run/watch`

Specimen `spec-test/sim-run/crates/sim-run/tests/watch` is checked by `cargo test`.

Source `crates/sim-run/tests/watch.rs`:

```rust
use std::process::Command;

// conformance: watch command dry-runs modeled and import boot plans.

#[test]
fn watch_modeled_dry_run_boots_headless() {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_sim"))
        .args([
            "watch",
            "--profile",
            "watch-glance",
            "--source",
            "modeled",
            "--fleet",
            "one",
            "--dry-run",
        ])
        .output()
        .expect("run sim watch dry-run");

    assert_eq!(output.status.code(), Some(0));
    assert!(output.stderr.is_empty());
    assert_eq!(
        String::from_utf8(output.stdout).unwrap(),
        "watch: profile=watch-glance tier=sensor+actuator source=modeled route=modeled\n\
watch: rate=1/1hz stale=hold-last glance-adapter=configured(device)\n\
watch: boot plan ready\n"
    );
}

#[test]
fn watch_import_dry_run_defaults_to_import_route() {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_sim"))
        .args(["watch", "--source", "import", "worn.jsonl", "--dry-run"])
        .output()
        .expect("run sim watch import dry-run");

    assert_eq!(output.status.code(), Some(0));
    assert!(output.stderr.is_empty());
    let stdout = String::from_utf8(output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(stdout.contains("source=import route=import"), "{stdout}");
    assert!(stdout.contains("watch: boot plan ready"), "{stdout}");
}

#[test]
fn watch_live_dry_run_names_unavailable_lane() {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_sim"))
        .args(["watch", "--source", "live", "--route", "ble", "--dry-run"])
        .output()
        .expect("run sim watch live dry-run");

    assert_eq!(output.status.code(), Some(0));
    assert!(output.stderr.is_empty());
    let stdout = String::from_utf8(output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(
        stdout.contains("route=ble reason=live provider is not installed in this build"),
        "{stdout}"
    );
    assert!(
        stdout.contains("source=live reason=--consent is required"),
        "{stdout}"
    );
}
```

### `feature/sim-run/glasses`

Specimen `spec-test/sim-run/crates/sim-run/tests/glasses` is checked by `cargo test`.

Source `crates/sim-run/tests/glasses.rs`:

```rust
use std::process::Command;

// conformance: glasses command dry-runs modeled and hardware boot plans.

#[test]
fn glasses_both_dry_run_boots_co_use_plan() {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_sim"))
        .args(["glasses", "--device", "both", "--pose", "auto", "--dry-run"])
        .output()
        .expect("run sim glasses dry-run");

    assert_eq!(output.status.code(), Some(0));
    assert!(output.stderr.is_empty());
    assert_eq!(
        String::from_utf8(output.stdout).unwrap(),
        "glasses: viture profile=luma-ultra tier=rich adapter=reprojector loop=60/120 stale=predict+clamp\n\
glasses: halo   profile=halo tier=actuator adapter=glance loop=5/30 stale=hold-last\n\
glasses: co-use hub ready; boot plan ready\n"
    );
}

#[test]
fn glasses_unverified_real_route_falls_back_to_modeled() {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_sim"))
        .args([
            "glasses",
            "--device",
            "halo",
            "--route",
            "ble-direct",
            "--dry-run",
        ])
        .output()
        .expect("run sim glasses hardware dry-run");

    assert_eq!(output.status.code(), Some(0));
    assert!(output.stderr.is_empty());
    let stdout = String::from_utf8(output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(
        stdout.contains("glasses: halo   route=ble-direct fallback=modeled reason=lane halo_ble_direct not verified"),
        "{stdout}"
    );
    assert!(stdout.contains("glasses: boot plan ready"), "{stdout}");
}

#[test]
fn glasses_auto_mirror_runs_headless_modeled() {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_sim"))
        .args(["glasses", "--device", "auto", "--mirror", "--dry-run"])
        .output()
        .expect("run sim glasses mirror dry-run");

    assert_eq!(output.status.code(), Some(0));
    assert!(output.stderr.is_empty());
    assert_eq!(
        String::from_utf8(output.stdout).unwrap(),
        "glasses: modeled profile=display-only tier=display adapter=mirror loop=1/1 stale=hold-last\n\
glasses: boot plan ready\n"
    );
}
```

### `feature/sim-run/loaders`

Specimen `spec-test/sim-run/crates/sim-run-loaders/src/native/codec_proxy_tests` is checked by `cargo test`.

Source `crates/sim-run-loaders/src/native/codec_proxy_tests.rs`:

```rust
use std::sync::Arc;

use sim_kernel::{Cx, DefaultFactory, Expr, NoopEvalPolicy, ReadPolicy, Symbol, WriteCx};

use super::{NativeAbiCodecDecoder, NativeAbiCodecEncoder, NativeGuest};

// conformance: native loader codec proxy preserves manifest and export payloads.

// A stand-in guest decodes the marshaled frame and returns a canned response,
// so the proxy's marshal/unmarshal bridge is exercised without a real dylib.
struct MockGuest;

impl NativeGuest for MockGuest {
    fn invoke(&self, op: &str, args: &[u8]) -> sim_kernel::Result<Vec<u8>> {
        let (_, expr) = sim_codec_binary::decode_frame(sim_kernel::CodecId(0), args)?;
        if op.ends_with("/decode") {
            assert!(
                matches!(expr, Expr::String(_)),
                "decode op should receive the input text as Expr::String"
            );
            Ok(sim_codec_binary::encode_frame(&Expr::Symbol(Symbol::new("parsed")))?.0)
        } else {
            Ok(sim_codec_binary::encode_frame(&Expr::String("rendered".to_owned()))?.0)
        }
    }
}

fn test_cx() -> Cx {
    Cx::new(Arc::new(NoopEvalPolicy), Arc::new(DefaultFactory))
}

#[test]
fn proxy_decoder_marshals_text_to_guest_and_returns_expr() {
    let decoder = NativeAbiCodecDecoder {
        guest: Arc::new(MockGuest),
        op: "codec/mock/decode".to_owned(),
    };
    let mut cx = test_cx();
    let mut rcx = sim_codec::ReadCx {
        cx: &mut cx,
        codec: sim_kernel::CodecId(0),
        read_policy: ReadPolicy::default(),
        limits: sim_codec::DecodeLimits::default(),
    };
    let expr = sim_codec::Decoder::decode(
        &decoder,
        &mut rcx,
        sim_codec::Input::Text("(hello)".to_owned()),
    )
    .unwrap();
    assert_eq!(expr, Expr::Symbol(Symbol::new("parsed")));
}

#[test]
fn proxy_encoder_marshals_expr_to_guest_and_returns_text() {
    let encoder = NativeAbiCodecEncoder {
        guest: Arc::new(MockGuest),
        op: "codec/mock/encode".to_owned(),
    };
    let mut cx = test_cx();
    let mut wcx = WriteCx {
        cx: &mut cx,
        codec: sim_kernel::CodecId(0),
        options: sim_kernel::EncodeOptions::default(),
    };
    let out =
        sim_codec::Encoder::encode(&encoder, &mut wcx, &Expr::Symbol(Symbol::new("x"))).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(out, sim_codec::Output::Text("rendered".to_owned()));
}
```

### `feature/sim-run/index-table-dir`

Specimen `spec-test/sim-run/crates/sim-lib-index/src/dir` is checked by `cargo test`.

Source `crates/sim-lib-index/src/dir.rs`:

```rust
//! Immutable Table/Dir view over an [`IndexDoc`](sim_index_core::IndexDoc).

use std::sync::Arc;

use sim_index_core::{DiscoveredSpecimen, IndexDoc};
use sim_kernel::{
    Cx, Dir, Error, Expr, Object, ObjectCompat, Result, Symbol, Table, Value,
    id::CORE_TABLE_CLASS_ID, object::ClassRef,
};

/// Immutable directory wrapper for the embedded SIM Index graph.
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct IndexDir {
    doc: Arc<IndexDoc>,
}

impl IndexDir {
    /// Builds a directory over `doc`.
    pub fn new(doc: IndexDoc) -> Self {
        Self { doc: Arc::new(doc) }
    }

    /// Borrows the wrapped document.
    pub fn doc(&self) -> &IndexDoc {
        &self.doc
    }
}

impl Object for IndexDir {
    fn display(&self, _cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<String> {
        Ok(format!(
            "index-dir[subjects={}, features={}, specimens={}]",
            self.doc.subjects.len(),
            self.doc.features.len(),
            self.doc.specimens.len()
        ))
    }

    fn as_any(&self) -> &dyn std::any::Any {
        self
    }
}

impl ObjectCompat for IndexDir {
    fn class(&self, cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<ClassRef> {
        let symbol = Symbol::qualified("core", "Table");
        if let Some(value) = cx.registry().class_by_symbol(&symbol) {
            return Ok(value.clone());
        }
        cx.factory().class_stub(CORE_TABLE_CLASS_ID, symbol)
    }

    fn as_expr(&self, cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<Expr> {
        self.as_table_expr(cx)
    }

    fn as_table_impl(&self) -> Option<&dyn Table> {
        Some(self)
    }

    fn as_dir(&self) -> Option<&dyn Dir> {
        Some(self)
    }
}

impl Table for IndexDir {
    fn backend_symbol(&self) -> Symbol {
        Symbol::qualified("index", "Dir")
    }

    fn get(&self, cx: &mut Cx, key: Symbol) -> Result<Value> {
        collection(cx, &self.doc, &key.to_string())?.map_or_else(|| cx.factory().nil(), Ok)
    }

    fn set(&self, _cx: &mut Cx, _key: Symbol, _value: Value) -> Result<()> {
        Err(immutable_error())
    }

    fn has(&self, _cx: &mut Cx, key: Symbol) -> Result<bool> {
        Ok(collection_name(&key.to_string()).is_some())
    }

    fn del(&self, _cx: &mut Cx, _key: Symbol) -> Result<Value> {
        Err(immutable_error())
    }

    fn keys(&self, _cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<Vec<Symbol>> {
        Ok(collection_names().map(Symbol::new).collect())
    }

    fn entries(&self, cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<Vec<(Symbol, Value)>> {
        collection_names()
            .map(|name| Ok((Symbol::new(name), collection(cx, &self.doc, name)?.unwrap())))
            .collect()
    }

    fn len(&self, _cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<usize> {
        Ok(collection_names().count())
    }

    fn clear(&self, _cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<()> {
        Err(immutable_error())
    }
}

impl Dir for IndexDir {
    fn mkdir(&self, _cx: &mut Cx, _name: Symbol) -> Result<Value> {
        Err(immutable_error())
    }

    fn opendir(&self, cx: &mut Cx, name: Symbol) -> Result<Option<Value>> {
        collection(cx, &self.doc, &name.to_string())
    }

    fn rmdir(&self, _cx: &mut Cx, _name: Symbol) -> Result<Value> {
        Err(immutable_error())
    }

    fn is_dir(&self, _cx: &mut Cx, name: Symbol) -> Result<bool> {
        Ok(collection_name(&name.to_string()).is_some())
    }
}

fn collection_names() -> impl Iterator<Item = &'static str> {
    [
        "subjects",
        "anchors",
        "surfaces",
        "specimens",
        "features",
        "routes",
        "edges",
    ]
    .into_iter()
}

fn collection_name(name: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
    collection_names().find(|candidate| *candidate == name)
}

fn collection(cx: &mut Cx, doc: &IndexDoc, name: &str) -> Result<Option<Value>> {
    let Some(name) = collection_name(name) else {
        return Ok(None);
    };
    let entries = match name {
        "subjects" => doc
            .subjects
            .iter()
            .map(|subject| {
                row(
                    subject.id.as_str(),
                    vec![
                        ("id", string(cx, subject.id.as_str())?),
                        ("kind", string(cx, &subject.kind)?),
                        ("title", string(cx, &subject.title)?),
                    ],
                    cx,
                )
            })
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        "anchors" => doc
            .anchors
            .iter()
            .map(|anchor| {
                row(
                    anchor.id.as_str(),
                    vec![
                        ("id", string(cx, anchor.id.as_str())?),
                        ("subject", string(cx, anchor.subject.as_str())?),
                        ("kind", string(cx, &anchor.kind)?),
                    ],
                    cx,
                )
            })
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        "surfaces" => doc
            .surfaces
            .iter()
            .map(|surface| {
                row(
                    surface.id.as_str(),
                    vec![
                        ("id", string(cx, surface.id.as_str())?),
                        ("subject", string(cx, surface.subject.as_str())?),
                        ("kind", string(cx, &surface.kind)?),
                    ],
                    cx,
                )
            })
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        "specimens" => doc
            .specimens
            .iter()
            .map(|specimen| specimen_row(cx, specimen))
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        "features" => doc
            .features
            .iter()
            .map(|feature| {
                row(
                    feature.id.as_str(),
                    vec![
                        ("id", string(cx, feature.id.as_str())?),
                        ("subject", string(cx, feature.subject.as_str())?),
                        ("title", string(cx, &feature.title)?),
                        ("summary", string(cx, &feature.summary)?),
                        ("surfaces", string_list(cx, feature.surfaces.iter())?),
                        ("specimens", string_list(cx, feature.specimens.iter())?),
                    ],
                    cx,
                )
            })
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        "routes" => doc
            .routes
            .iter()
            .map(|route| {
                row(
                    route.id.as_str(),
                    vec![
                        ("id", string(cx, route.id.as_str())?),
                        ("title", string(cx, &route.title)?),
                        ("steps", string(cx, &route.steps.len().to_string())?),
                    ],
                    cx,
                )
            })
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        "edges" => doc
            .edges
            .iter()
            .enumerate()
            .map(|(index, edge)| {
                row(
                    &index.to_string(),
                    vec![
                        ("from", string(cx, &edge.from)?),
                        ("rel", string(cx, &edge.rel)?),
                        ("to", string(cx, &edge.to)?),
                    ],
                    cx,
                )
            })
            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?,
        _ => Vec::new(),
    };
    Ok(Some(cx.factory().table(entries)?))
}

fn specimen_row(cx: &mut Cx, specimen: &DiscoveredSpecimen) -> Result<(Symbol, Value)> {
    row(
        specimen.id.as_str(),
        vec![
            ("id", string(cx, specimen.id.as_str())?),
            ("subject", string(cx, specimen.subject.as_str())?),
            ("kind", string(cx, &specimen.kind)?),
            ("path", string(cx, &specimen.path)?),
            (
                "language",
                optional_string(cx, specimen.language.as_deref())?,
            ),
            ("runnable", cx.factory().bool(specimen.runnable)?),
            ("checked", cx.factory().bool(specimen.checked)?),
            (
                "checked_by",
                optional_string(cx, specimen.checked_by.as_deref())?,
            ),
        ],
        cx,
    )
}

fn row(id: &str, entries: Vec<(&str, Value)>, cx: &mut Cx) -> Result<(Symbol, Value)> {
    let row_entries = entries
        .into_iter()
        .map(|(key, value)| (Symbol::new(key), value))
        .collect();
    Ok((Symbol::new(id), cx.factory().table(row_entries)?))
}

fn string(cx: &mut Cx, value: &str) -> Result<Value> {
    cx.factory().string(value.to_owned())
}

fn optional_string(cx: &mut Cx, value: Option<&str>) -> Result<Value> {
    match value {
        Some(value) => string(cx, value),
        None => cx.factory().nil(),
    }
}

fn string_list<'a, I, T>(cx: &mut Cx, values: I) -> Result<Value>
where
    I: IntoIterator<Item = &'a T>,
    T: ToString + 'a,
{
    let values = values
        .into_iter()
        .map(|value| string(cx, &value.to_string()))
        .collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?;
    cx.factory().list(values)
}

fn immutable_error() -> Error {
    Error::Eval("SIM Index Dir is immutable".to_owned())
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use std::sync::Arc;

    use sim_index_core::Visibility;
    use sim_kernel::{DefaultFactory, NoopEvalPolicy};

    use super::*;

    // conformance: index Table/Dir backend exposes immutable generated index rows.
    #[test]
    fn table_dir_exposes_known_collections() {
        let mut cx = Cx::new(Arc::new(NoopEvalPolicy), Arc::new(DefaultFactory));
        let dir = IndexDir::new(IndexDoc {
            schema: "sim.index".to_owned(),
            generated_by: "test".to_owned(),
            visibility: Visibility::Public,
            subjects: Vec::new(),
            anchors: Vec::new(),
            surfaces: Vec::new(),
            specimens: Vec::new(),
            drafts: Vec::new(),
            features: Vec::new(),
            routes: Vec::new(),
            edges: Vec::new(),
        });

        assert_eq!(dir.len(&mut cx).unwrap(), 7);
        assert!(dir.has(&mut cx, Symbol::new("features")).unwrap());
        assert!(dir.is_dir(&mut cx, Symbol::new("specimens")).unwrap());
        assert!(!dir.has(&mut cx, Symbol::new("missing")).unwrap());
        assert_eq!(
            dir.display(&mut cx).unwrap(),
            "index-dir[subjects=0, features=0, specimens=0]"
        );
        let nil = cx.factory().nil().unwrap();
        assert!(dir.set(&mut cx, Symbol::new("features"), nil).is_err());
    }
}
```

### `feature/sim-run/terminal-surface`

Specimen `recipe/sim-run/02-scenarios/host-verb` is checked by `xtask check-recipes`.

Source `recipes/02-scenarios/host-verb/recipe.toml`:

```toml
id = "host-verb"
title = "Host library verb"
codec = "shell"
setup = "setup.sh"
purpose = "purpose.md"
order = 20
tags = ["cli", "host", "verb", "offline", "deterministic"]
requires = ["sim-run-core", "host:scenario/host"]
network = false
```
